title: Закон за електронния документ и електронните удостоверителни услуги (ЗЕДЕУУ)
title_en: Electronic Document and Electronic Certification Services Act
description: Governs electronic documents, electronic signatures, and trust services in Bulgaria, implementing Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 (eIDAS Regulation). Establishes the legal framework for electronic identification, electronic signatures, electronic seals, time stamps, and electronic delivery services.

provisions.title: Subject and scope
provisions.content: This Act regulates electronic documents, electronic identification, electronic signatures, electronic seals, electronic time stamps, and trust services. This Act supplements the directly applicable provisions of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 23 July 2014 on electronic identification and trust services for electronic transactions in the internal market and repealing Directive 1999/93/EC (eIDAS Regulation).

provisions.title: Electronic document defined
provisions.content: An electronic document shall be an electronic statement that is recorded on a magnetic, optical or other medium enabling the reproduction of the statement. An electronic document meeting the requirements of this Act and signed with a qualified electronic signature shall have the same legal force as a signed written document. An electronic document is considered to have been presented to an addressee when it has reached his or her information system.

provisions.title: Legal validity
provisions.content: An electronic document shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form. A qualified electronic signature shall have the equivalent legal effect of a handwritten signature in accordance with Article 25(2) of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The effect of other types of electronic signatures shall be determined by an agreement between the parties.

provisions.title: Types of electronic signatures
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act, the following types of electronic signatures shall be used: simple electronic signature — data in electronic form which are attached to or logically associated with other electronic data and which serve as a method of authentication; advanced electronic signature — an electronic signature meeting the requirements of Article 26 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014; qualified electronic signature — an advanced electronic signature created by a qualified electronic signature creation device, based on a qualified certificate for electronic signatures.

provisions.title: Qualified certificates
provisions.content: A qualified certificate for electronic signatures is a certificate for electronic signatures that meets the requirements laid down in Annex I to Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 and is issued by a qualified trust service provider. Qualified certificates shall contain: the signatory's name or pseudonym; an indication that the certificate has been issued as a qualified certificate; the data used for validation of the electronic signature; the identity and location of the trust service provider.

provisions.title: Qualified trust service providers
provisions.content: A qualified trust service provider is a trust service provider that has been granted qualified status by the supervisory body in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. Qualified trust service providers shall meet the requirements set out in Article 24 of the Regulation, including: employ suitable staff with necessary expertise; use trustworthy systems and products; take appropriate measures against forgery and theft of data; verify the identity of applicants for qualified certificates.

provisions.title: Supervisory body
provisions.content: The Communications Regulation Commission shall be the supervisory body for trust service providers in the Republic of Bulgaria within the meaning of Article 17 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. The supervisory body shall: supervise qualified trust service providers; take action against non-qualified trust service providers when informed of non-compliance; maintain and publish the trusted list of qualified trust service providers.

provisions.title: Electronic identification scheme
provisions.content: The Republic of Bulgaria shall participate in the mutual recognition of electronic identification means in accordance with Chapter II of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. Electronic identification means issued under a notified electronic identification scheme of another Member State shall be recognised in the Republic of Bulgaria for the purpose of cross-border authentication. The assurance levels of electronic identification means shall be: low, substantial, and high.

provisions.title: National electronic identification
provisions.content: The national electronic identification scheme shall be established and operated by the State e-Government Agency. The national electronic identification means shall provide a high level of assurance and shall be based on the Bulgarian personal identity card with an electronic identifier. The scheme shall enable citizens to identify themselves securely when using electronic services provided by public administration bodies.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 5 - Electronic Seals and Time Stamps
provisions.title: Electronic seals
provisions.content: An electronic seal shall serve as evidence that an electronic document was issued by a legal person and ensures the origin and integrity of the document. A qualified electronic seal shall enjoy the presumption of integrity of the data and of correctness of the origin of that data to which the qualified electronic seal is linked, in accordance with Article 35 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 5 - Electronic Seals and Time Stamps
provisions.title: Electronic time stamps
provisions.content: An electronic time stamp shall link data in electronic form to a particular time, establishing evidence that those data existed at that time. A qualified electronic time stamp shall enjoy the presumption of the accuracy of the date and the time it indicates and the integrity of the data to which the date and time are bound, in accordance with Article 41 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014.

provisions.title: Electronic delivery
provisions.content: A qualified electronic registered delivery service shall provide evidence relating to the handling of the transmitted data, including proof of sending and receiving the data, and shall protect transmitted data against the risk of loss, theft, damage or any unauthorised alterations, in accordance with Article 44 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014. Qualified electronic registered delivery services may be used for legally binding communications with public administration bodies.

provisions.title: Trust service provider liability
provisions.content: A qualified trust service provider shall be liable for damages caused to any natural or legal person due to failure to comply with its obligations under Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 and this Act. Unless the qualified trust service provider proves that it has not acted negligently, it shall be liable for all damages caused. The liability provisions shall apply in accordance with Article 13 of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014.

provisions.title: Administrative sanctions
provisions.content: A trust service provider that fails to comply with the requirements of Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 or this Act shall be subject to a fine of BGN 5,000 to BGN 50,000. A trust service provider that issues a qualified certificate without properly verifying the identity of the applicant shall be subject to a fine of BGN 10,000 to BGN 100,000. For repeated violations, the fine shall be doubled.

definitions.definition: An electronic statement that is recorded on a magnetic, optical or other medium enabling the reproduction of the statement.
An advanced electronic signature created by a qualified electronic signature creation device, based on a qualified certificate for electronic signatures.
A trust service provider that has been granted qualified status by the supervisory body in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 910/2014.
